english_query: what it means to live a good life by being a good person
ugf_response:
To live a life that feels good by being a good person, think of it as a set of small steps that fit together like parts of a day. 

First, we see that a good person tries to make things better for other people. This can be as simple as giving a smile, sharing a small thing to play with, or listening when a friend talks. When we do these things, we help the other person feel safe and happy, and we also feel a warm feeling inside. 

Second, a good person tries to be just. This means not taking more than a just share, not saying things that are not true, and not hurting other people feelings on a set idea. If we see a chance to take something that does not belong to us, we think about how we would feel if we were the one who lost it, and we choose to give it back or leave it. 

Third, a good person looks after the things that matter to them, like family, friends, and the place they live in. This can be done by helping a parent with home work, calling a friend who seems sad, or picking up things that are not needed on a walk. By caring for these things, we keep them strong and can enjoy them for a long time. 

The next step after the third is that a good person learns from what happens. If we do something wrong, we think about why it happened, say sorry if we hurt someone, and try to do better next time. This keeps us from falling into the same way of doing wrong things and helps us grow. 

The next step after the next step after the third is that a good person tries to stay true to what they feel is right inside. When we feel a pull to do something that might hurt another, we stop and ask if that feeling is the same as the warm feeling we get when we help. If it is not, we choose the other path. 

When all these steps are put together, the life that follows feels full and good. It is not about having a lot of things or being given thanks by many, but about feeling that we have done what we can to make the world a little kinder and that we have been kind to me and my own. By being this kind of person, each day adds a small piece to a life that feels good, safe, and full of warm feelings.

english_query: If the meeting starts at noon, then everyone arrives before noon. The meeting starts at noon. Therefore, everyone arrives before noon. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the meeting starts at the middle of the day, then everyone gets there before that time. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us the meeting does start at the middle of the day. This fits the start of the first idea. Since the start part is true, the end part must be true too. So, everyone must get there before the middle of the day. We can think of a small story to check this. If the first idea is right, there is no way for the meeting to start at the middle of the day and for people to be late. So the last idea is true for sure. follows

english_query: If the bird is a robin, then the bird has feathers. The bird has feathers. Therefore, the bird is a robin. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the bird is a red bird, then it has small soft things on its body. This means being a red bird is one way to have these soft things. Then, we look at the second idea. It tells us that this bird has these soft things. We need to decide if this means the bird is a red bird. Let us think about other birds. A yellow bird has these soft things. A white bird has these soft things. So, just because a bird has these soft things, it does not have to be a red bird. It could be many other kinds of birds. The first idea does not say that only red birds have these soft things. It only says red birds do have them. So, we can imagine a story where the bird is a yellow bird. In that story, the first idea is true because red birds have these soft things. The second idea is true because the yellow bird has these soft things. But the last idea is not true because the bird is not a red bird. Since we can have a story where the early ideas are true but the last idea is not, the last idea does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: how some ideas stop you from thinking further
ugf_response:
Person number one: I want to know why some ways of thinking make it hard to find new ways of thinking.

Person number two: It is because your mind can get stuck in a small space. When you think a certain way, your brain stays in that space and does not want to leave.

Person number one: Why does it stay there?

Person number two: Because the way you already think feels good and easy. It is like a path in the woods. Once you walk the same path many times, it is easy to follow. You do not look for new paths because the old one is right there.

Person number one: Does this mean I cannot see what is real if I am stuck?

Person number two: Yes. If you only look through one window, you will only see one part of the world. You might think you see everything, but you are missing the rest.

Person number one: How can I stop this from happening to me?

Person number two: You must try to look through many windows. You must ask yourself if what you think is the only way that is real. You must try to think in a way that feels hard or strange. This helps you break out of that small space.

english_query: It looks like the hidden sending of the god part is not just about the gift of making right. For sending a god part means giving that part. So if the god part is sent only because of the gift of making right, then the god part itself is not given, but only its gifts; and this is the wrong idea of those who say the spirit from god is not given, but only its gifts are.
ugf_response: By the gift of making right, the thinking thing is made whole so that it can use not only the made gift, but also be with the god part itself; and so the hidden sending happens because of the gift of making right; and yet the god part itself is given.

english_query: Also, some jobs belong to the groups of sky helpers, like to watch over, to do great signs, to stop bad spirits, and so on; which do not seem to belong to the souls of good men. So they are not moved into the sky helper groups.
ugf_response: Sky helpers are by how things are made between us and god; and so by the usual way they take care of not only human things, but all body things. But good men even after this life are of the same kind as us; and so by the usual way they do not take care of human things, 'nor do they get into the things of the living,' as the great old church teacher says. Still, by a special giving it is sometimes let to some of the good men to do these jobs; by doing great signs, by stopping bad spirits, or by doing something of that kind, as the great old church teacher says.

english_query: the way to help yourself think about hard questions step by step
ugf_response:
First idea they say is that when you face a hard question you should first find a part of it that you know is real. They tell you to look for a fact that you can see with your own mind or with a place you trust. This part is taken as the real start point that you can hold on to while you move forward.

Second idea they say comes after that real start point. From the real part they say you should try to see what next part might follow. They ask you to ask yourself if one thing makes another thing seem more true, or if one step makes the next step easier to see. In this way they want you to build a small group of thoughts that goes from the real start point toward a possible answer.

Third idea they say is that you should look at the group you have built and ask if the way you have moved makes sense. They say you should check if each part that joins feels right, if it helps you get closer to a clear answer, and if it does not hurt the other parts you already know. If the group feels right and helps you see a clear answer, then the thinking is good. If it feels off, makes you more confused, or seems to push you away from the real start point, then the thinking is not good and you should go back and try a new way. This three part way is meant to give you a simple road to think about hard questions step by step.

english_query: Either the package arrives today or the package arrives tomorrow. The package arrives today. Therefore, the package does not arrive tomorrow. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says one of two things must be true. Either the box comes here today, or the box comes here tomorrow. This is a choice between two times. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us for sure that the box comes here today. Now we check if the last idea must be true. The last idea says the box does not come tomorrow. We need to see if the first idea means only one of the two things can happen. The word 'or' in normal talk often means you can have both. For a case, if I say 'I will eat a red thing or a yellow thing', I might eat both. The first idea does not say 'either... or... but not both'. It just says at least one is true. So, it is possible that the box comes today AND the box comes tomorrow. If the box comes both days, then the first idea is still true. And the second idea is still true. But the last idea would be wrong because the box did come tomorrow. Since we can imagine a time when the first two ideas are right but the last one is wrong, the last idea does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: how to think about what could happen and how likely it is
ugf_response:
Imagine you are sitting in a quiet room and you try to think about a thing that may happen later. You want to know what may happen and how many times it may happen. This is a hard thing to think about because the future is not set like a story that we have already read.

The hard thing is to find a way to think about how many times something may happen. First, some people say we should look at what has happened before and use that to guess what may happen next. They think that if something has happened many times in the past, it may happen many times again. Second, some people say we should think about all the different ways it could happen and add up all of them. They feel that the more ways there are, the more often it may happen. Third, some people say we should trust a feeling inside us, a deep feeling, and not try to add up at all. They think that a feeling can tell us when something will happen more often.

Why do people give these different replies? The first group trusts what we have seen, because what we see gives us a picture of what has been true before. The second group likes to think about many possible ways, because they want to be sure they have not missed a way that could change the whole number of things. The third group trusts a feeling because they think a feeling can see things that numbers cannot, and they do not want to be stuck in a long line of past pictures.

Each reply has a reason that makes sense to the person who says it. The first one feels safe because it rests on what we have already seen. The second one feels right because it tries to add up all the ways. The third one feels quick because it does not need a long line to be made. In the end, the hard thing stays the same: we must choose a way to think about what may happen and how many times it may happen, and we must live with the fact that no way can give us the very same answer for every case.

english_query: Also, seeing something better can happen in two ways: because of the thing seen, or because of the power of the one who sees. On the side of the thing, it happens because the thing is taken in better, that is, by a better picture; but this does not fit here, for God is there for the mind seeing Him not by a picture, but by His true being. So if one sees Him better than another, it must be because of the change in mind power; so the one with more mind power will see Him clearer; and this is wrong; since being like the good spirits is promised to men as their great good.
ugf_response: The change in seeing will not come from the thing seen, for the same thing will be shown to all--that is, the true being of God; nor will it come from different ways of taking in the thing by different pictures; but it will come from the different power of the mind, not the normal power, but the power made great.

english_query: Also, the life of doing is said to be not the same as the life of thinking. But the life of thinking is only different from the life of doing by some acts. So life is an act.
ugf_response: By acts of life we mean those whose start is inside the one who does them, and by which the one who does them makes such acts by himself. It is true that in men there are not just the born starts of some acts, which are their born powers, but something more than these, like ways of being that make them want to do certain acts as if by a second way, so that the acts bring good feel. So, like a picture, any work in which a man finds good feel, so that his wish is for it, his time is spent on it, and his whole life is set for it, is called the life of that man. So some are said to live a life of bad want, others a life of good. In this way the life of thinking is not the same as the life of doing, and so to know God is called life forever. So the answer to the third point is clear.

english_query: how two things happening together does not mean one causes the other
ugf_response:
Imagine two things that happen at the same time. Let us say that every time the sun comes up, a man in a small house opens his window. If we see the sun come up and then see the man open his window, we might think that the sun makes the man open his window. We might think that the sun is the thing that makes the window open. This is a way that our minds work. We see two things together and we want to say that the first thing made the second thing happen.

But this is not always the truth. There is a hard question we must ask. Does the first thing really make the second thing happen, or are they just happening together for another reason?

One way to look at this is to think about a third thing. Let us say that it is morning. The morning makes the sun come up, and the morning also makes the man wake up and open his window. In this way, the sun and the window are happening together, but the sun did not make the window open. The morning made both things happen. The two things are together, but one did not make the other.

Other people might say that it is just a chance that they happen together. They might say that if you watch for a long time, you will see many things that happen at the same time that have no way of being joined at all. It is just how the world is.

We must be very careful when we see two things together. We cannot say that one makes the other happen just because they are seen at the same time. We must look for the thing that makes them both happen, or we must see if one can really make the other. If we do not do this, we will think things are true when they are not.

english_query: If the engine has fuel, then the car can move. The engine has fuel. Therefore, the car can move. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea which says if the engine has the stuff that makes it run, then the car can move. This sets up a clear tie between having that stuff and being able to move. Then, we look at the second idea which tells us the engine does have that stuff. Since the first part of our if-then tie is true, the second part must also be true. So, we can say the car can move. follows

english_query: Either the cake is chocolate or the cake is vanilla. The cake is chocolate. Therefore, the cake is not vanilla. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we notice the first idea says the sweet food is either brown or white. Then, we see the second idea says the sweet food is brown. Since the sweet food is brown, it cannot be white. So the last idea that the sweet food is not white follows for sure. follows

english_query: If the bridge is open, then traffic can cross the river. Traffic can cross the river. Therefore, the bridge is open. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea: if the bridge is open, then cars can go over the water. Then, we see the second idea says that cars are going over the water. But we must ask if the bridge is the only way for cars to go over the water. Maybe there is a big boat that carries cars. The first idea does not say that the bridge is the only way. So, just because cars are crossing does not mean the bridge must be open. The last idea does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: why being sure about something does not make it right
ugf_response:
A person can feel very, very sure about a thing. They might feel it deep inside their heart or in their head. They might see things that make them think they are right. They might have a lot of things that seem to show their way of thinking is the only way. This feeling of being sure is very strong. It can make a person feel like they have found the truth.

But here is the hard question. Does the way a person feels about a thing change if that thing is true or not? If a person feels all of the ways possible that they are right that the moon is made of a soft white food that people eat, does their feeling make the moon that food? No. The moon is not that food. The feeling does not change what the moon is.

Some people might say that if enough people feel sure, then it must be true. They might think that if a whole group of people all believe the same thing with a lot of power in their belief, that thing becomes real for them. They think that the power of many people believing together can change the world.

Other people will say that truth does not care about how people feel. They will say that truth is something that stays the same whether we know it or not. They will say that a person can be very sure and be very wrong at the same time. They will say that we must look at the things themselves and not at how we feel about them. They will say that being sure is just a feeling in our heads and does not touch the real world.

The problem is that being sure can stop us from looking for more truth. When we think we are sure, we stop asking questions. We stop looking for new things that might show us we are wrong. This is why being sure can be a bad thing. It can make us close our eyes to what is actually happening.

english_query: If he turns on the light, then the room is bright. He does not turn on the light. Therefore, the room is not bright. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if he turns on the light, then the room is bright. This tells us what happens when the light goes on. It does not say what happens if the light stays off. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us he does not turn on the light. So the part about turning on the light did not happen. Now we ask if the room must be dark. The first idea only tells us about the time when the light is on. It leaves open the chance that the room is bright for other reasons. Maybe the sun is giving light through a window. Maybe another light is on. All of these things could make the room bright even if he did not touch the thing that turns on the light. So, we can agree with the first two ideas and still say the room is bright. This means the last idea does not have to be true. It is possible for the room to be bright even if he did not turn on the light. So the last idea does not follow for sure from the earlier ones. does not follow

english_query: The decision reflects mental accounting: the farmer treats grain from different sources as if they were not interchangeable. In economics, all bushels are fungible, so the differing risk attitudes are irrational. Review the definition of mental accounting and the House Money Effect.
ugf_response: First, we look at the person who grows food. He sees the food he grew as different from food he bought. He puts them in different boxes in his head. Next, we think about the idea that money won in a game feels less real than money from work. The person feels his own food is harder to give up. Then, we ask if this is wrong. The study of money says all food is the same. So acting like they are different is not good thinking. But, the person feels pain losing what he grew. This feeling is real. Finally, we see that while he breaks the good thinking ways, his feelings explain what he does. It is a human way of being. The person acts like his own food is special and bought food is not. This shows how people keep things in different boxes in their heads even when they are the same.

english_query: Either the suspect was at home or the suspect was at work. The suspect was at home. Therefore, the suspect was not at work. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says the person was in one of two places: home or work. This means they could not be in both places at the same time. Next, the second idea tells us the person was at home. Since we know they were at home, and they can only be in one place, we can be sure they were not at work. So the last idea follows for sure from the first ideas. follows

english_query: How there can be wrong ideas--that hard part still bothers the eye of my mind; and I am not sure if I should stop asking the question, or start again in a new way.
ugf_response: Start again, the old teacher,--at least if you think that there is any need for doing so. Did not you and the friend just now say very well, that in talks of this kind we may take our own time?

english_query: It looks like these days are not counted right. For the work of making things is just as different from the work of sorting and dressing them up as those two works are from each other. But we give different days for sorting and dressing up, so we should give a different day for making things.
ugf_response: The great old church teacher says the work of making things is about making stuff without shape and spirit without shape, which are outside of time, as he says. So the making of either is put before any day. But we can also say, like other writers who write for god, that sorting and dressing up mean changes in things that we can tell by time; but making things is only the god act that brings things into being all at once. For this reason, we say every work of sorting and dressing up happens in a day, but making things happens in the beginning, which means something that cannot be cut into parts.

english_query: how some ideas stop you from thinking further
ugf_response:
Sometimes, a person has a way of thinking that stays the same. This way of thinking is like a box. When you are inside the box, you can only see what is inside the box. You might think you are seeing the whole world, but you are only seeing a small part of it. This is a problem because it stops you from finding new things or new ways to be right. This can make it so you cannot see things that are right in front of you.

The hard question is this: how can a person know when their own way of thinking is making it so they cannot see what is true? How can we see the walls of the box when we are inside it?

First, some people might say that we can find the truth by looking at what is happening around us. They say that if our way of thinking is wrong, the things we see in the world will not be the same as what we think. If we think the sun goes around the earth, but we see the sun move in a different way, then we know our thought was wrong. For these people, the world tells us when we are wrong.

Second, other people might say that we can only find the truth by talking to others. They think that if one person is in a box, they cannot see the walls. But if many people come together, they can see each other. If one person thinks one thing and another person thinks something else, they can look at the space between those two thoughts. By looking at what they both do not see, they might find the truth.

Third, still other people might say that we must look deep inside our own minds. They think we must watch how our own minds work. They say we must ask our own minds why we think a certain way. They think that if we are very careful and very slow, we can see the ways our minds try to stay safe and stay in the box. They believe that being very true to what is inside you is the only way to break the box.

english_query: That is what is needed, the well known teacher of speaking; show the short way now, and the long way at some other time.
ugf_response: Well, I will; and you will certainly say, that you never heard a man use less words.

english_query: when helping the group costs you but helps everyone
ugf_response:
Imagine a group of people who want to do something big together. To get this thing done, everyone must give something up. This might be their time, their money, or their own feeling of being well and easy.

First, we look at what happens to the person who gives. This person loses a part of what they have. They might feel tired or they might have less money to spend on themselves. This is a bad thing for that one person. It feels like a bad thing for them in that moment.

Second, we look at what happens to the whole group. Because this person gave something up, the group now has more power or more things to use. The thing that the group wants to do becomes possible. Because the group does what it wants to do, every person in the group gets something good.

Now we see the big idea. The person who gave something up has less, but the group has more. If every person in the group thinks only about themselves, no one will give anything. If no one gives anything, the group can never do the big thing. The group will stay small and not have much power.

But if people decide to give even when it is hard for them, the group becomes strong. The good thing for everyone is much bigger than the bad thing for the one person. This is how groups do things that are too big for just one person to do alone. It is a way to make the whole world better by asking each person to give a little bit of what they have.

english_query: how to think about what the other person will do before you act
ugf_response:
The person wants to tell us how to think before we do something. They want to know what the other person will do when we do a thing.

First, the part of what they say that is true is that we must think about the other person. This is a thing that is true. We must always think about what others will do. If we do a thing without thinking about them, we might make a bad choice.

Second, the part of what they say that they think follows from this is that if we think about the other person first, we will be able to make a better choice. They think that knowing what the other person might do will help us to get what we want or to avoid a bad thing.

Third, we look at if their thinking is good. To see if the thinking is good, we must see if the first part and the second part fit together in a way that makes sense. If we know what the other person will do, we can change how we do things in a real way. This part of the thinking is good. However, there is a problem. We can never know for sure what a person will do. We can only make a guess. If our guess is wrong, what we do might still be bad. So, the thinking is good in its way, but it is not perfect because we cannot see into the minds of others.

english_query: the problem of spending more just because you already spent a lot
ugf_response:
The first idea in this case is that a person has already given a lot of money or time to a thing. The second idea is that the person decides to give more money or time to the same thing just because they have already given so much. The person thinks that if they stop now, the first money or time was lost for no good reason. So they think the only good way is to keep going until the thing is done.

This thinking is not good. The money or time already spent is gone. It cannot come back. It should not change what we do next. We should only look at what will happen if we spend more now. If the new spending will not help us get what we want, we should stop. If we keep spending just to save the first spending, we will lose even more. This is a bad way to think because it makes us throw good money after bad. We should look at the future, not the past.

english_query: I will tell you, if you like.
ugf_response: I should very much like.

english_query: If the road is icy, then driving is dangerous. The road is icy. Therefore, driving is dangerous. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea which says if the road has ice, then driving is not safe. The next idea tells us the road has ice. Since the first part of the if-then thought is true, the second part must be true too. So this means driving is not safe. The last idea says driving is not safe. This is the same as what we found. So the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: If the fire has fuel, then the fire keeps burning. The fire has fuel. Therefore, the fire keeps burning. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First we see a line that says if the fire has thing that makes fire hot then the fire keeps burning. That says the first half is the fire has thing that makes fire hot and the second half is the fire keeps burning. Then we see a second line that says the fire has thing that makes fire hot. So the first half of the if then is true. In an if then when the first half is true the second half must also be true. So the fire keeps burning. That shows the last line is true for sure. So the last line is true. follows

english_query: Either the cake is chocolate or the cake is vanilla. The cake is chocolate. Therefore, the cake is not vanilla. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that the sweet food is one of two kinds. It is the dark brown kind or the white kind. This means it has to be one or the other. It cannot be both. It cannot be something else. Next, we look at the second idea. It tells us for sure that the sweet food is the dark brown kind. Since we know the sweet food is the dark brown kind, and we know from the first idea that it must be either the dark brown kind or the white kind, we can see what happens to the white kind part. If the dark brown part is true, the white kind part must be not true. So, the idea that the sweet food is not the white kind fits with what we know. If the sweet food is the dark brown kind, it cannot be the white kind. So this means the last idea follows for sure from the first two ideas. follows

english_query: I do not think that I can.
ugf_response: Listen, then, to what the other side says, which is said by the people who say things are just how they look. They would say, as I imagine, can that which is all different from something have the same thing as that from which it is different? and look, the bright young one, that the word 'other' means not 'a little,' but 'all different.'

english_query: If the engine has fuel, then the car can move. If the car can move, then the bank is flooded. Therefore, if the engine has fuel, then the bank is flooded. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says that if the engine has the stuff that makes it go, then the car can move. This shows a path from the stuff to the moving car. Second, we look at the next idea. It says that if the car can move, then the money place is under water. This shows a path from the moving car to the water in the money place. Now we put these two ideas together. If the engine has the stuff, the car moves. And if the car moves, the money place is under water. So, if the engine has the stuff, it leads to the car moving, which leads to the money place being under water. The last idea says that if the engine has the stuff, then the money place is under water. This fits our step by step thoughts. We can try a small story to see if this is right. Let us say the engine has the stuff. Because of the first idea, the car moves. Because of the second idea, since the car moves, the money place is under water. So the money place is under water. This shows that the last idea must be true if the first two are true. There is no way for the first two to be true and the last one to be wrong. So the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: It looks like when we see good and better, we also see bad and worse. Good and better are thought about by looking at what is the best. So bad and worse are thought about by looking at some most bad thing.
ugf_response: The power of any thing grows with what the thing is. And as a shape is a best thing, not full takes away a best thing. So each shape, best thing, and good thing get more strong the closer they get to the best end; but not full and bad get more strong the farther they get from that end. So we do not call a thing bad and worse because it is near the most bad, just like we call a thing good and better because it is near the most good.

english_query: Also, free choice means being able to pick between two ways. But the bright spirits do not have this power in their thinking; because, as was said before, their thinking is not wrong about things they can know by their own power. So they cannot have free choice in their wanting part either.
ugf_response: As was said before, knowing happens when the thing known is inside the one who knows. But the act of the wanting part comes from this, that the feeling is pointed at something outside. Yet having all parts in a thing does not come from everything it is drawn to, but only from something higher than it. So it does not show not having all parts in a bright spirit if its will is not set about things lower than it; but it would show not having all parts in it, if it were not set about what is higher than it.

english_query: Either the answer is yes or the answer is no. The answer is yes. Therefore, the answer is not no. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.

So we know that yes is true. From these two lines we can be sure that yes is true, but we do not have any line that says no cannot also be true. Because the first line does not say that the two choices cannot both be true, it is possible that both yes and no are true. So we cannot be sure that the answer is not no. The last line does not have to be true. does not follow

english_query: why following rules is not enough if you do not care about people
ugf_response: First, we must look at what it means to follow the ways people say we should act. We often have a set of ways that tell us what is right and what is wrong. If we only follow these ways because we must, we are just doing what we are told without thinking about the people around us. Second, we see that these ways are often made to keep things good for everyone. If a person follows the ways but does not care about others, they might find ways to do what is told while still making people feel great pain. Third, we see that a person can follow every way and still do things that make people feel very bad. To show this, a person might follow a way that says they must keep all their money, and they do this in a way that is the very same as what was asked, but they do it while watching other people die because they have no food to eat. Next, we see that the heart of what is good is not just in the ways we follow, but in how we feel about other living things. If we have no care for others, the ways we follow become empty and do not bring the good they were meant to bring. Finally, we can see that following the ways without care is not enough because the ways are only a way to help people, and if we do not care about people, the ways lose their true meaning.

english_query: the difference between a strong reason and a weak reason
ugf_response:
Imagine you want to do something. You tell a friend why you want to do it. This reason is a way to show that your choice is good or right. Sometimes, one reason is very strong and makes people want to agree with you. Other times, a reason is not strong and does not make sense to anyone.

The hard question is how we know which reasons are strong and which ones are not strong. How can we tell if a reason is good enough to make a choice?

One way to look at this is to see if the reason rests on things that are true for everyone. A strong reason might be something that we can see and touch, or something that stays the same for all people. To show you what I mean, if you say you must eat because your body needs food to live, that is a strong reason. It is a thing that is true for all living things. People will see this as a good way to think.

Another way to look at this is to see if the reason is just a feeling. A reason that is not strong might be something that only one person feels and cannot be shown to others. If you say you must eat because the color of the food makes you feel happy, some people might say that is a reason that is not strong. It is not something that stays true for everyone. It is just a feeling in your head.

Some people might say a reason is strong if it helps many people. They might think that if a choice makes the most people happy, then the reason for that choice is very good. They look at the end of the story to see if it was good.

Other people might say a reason is strong only if it follows a set of ways that we all agreed to follow before. They do not care as much about the end of the story. They care about whether the way you got there was right from the start. They look at the way you think to see if it is the same as the ways we have always used.

In the end, we are always trying to find the best ways to tell if our thoughts are good or if they are bad. We want to know if we are making a good choice or if we are just following a feeling that has no weight.

english_query: why treating people as things to use is always wrong
ugf_response: First, we see that each person has a mind that can feel a feeling of happy and can feel hurt and can want things for themselves. Then we see that when we act as if a person is just a thing to get what we want, we do not think about that mind or those feelings. This makes the person feel hurt and can make them feel less safe. Next, we see that when one person does this to another, the other may stop trusting the first person. Trust is the feeling that we can count on each other and that we can work together. When trust goes down, groups of many people find it hard to work together and get less good things for all. Then we see that if many people start to think that it is okay to use each other like things we use, the whole group will feel less happy and will have less help for each other. This also means that each person will have the taking away of a chance to make their own choices and to live in a way that feels good to them. Because of the hurt, the taking away of trust, the less happy group, and the taking away of the chance for each person to choose, using people as if they are just things to use is always bad. This shows why we should always think of each person as a mind with feelings and not just as a thing to get what we want.

english_query: when one person doing something first changes what others do
ugf_response:
The first part of this thought is what the person says is true. They say that when one person does a thing, it can make other people change what they do. This is the starting point. It is the thing they see as a fact in the world.

The second part is what they think comes after that first part. They think that if one person can change what others do, then we can see how people act together by looking at how one person acts alone. They think that the way one person moves can show us how the whole group will move.

The third part is whether the way they think is good. To see if the thinking is good, we must ask if the first part always leads to the second part. It might be true that one person changes others. But it might not be true that we can know everything about the group just by looking at one person. People are not like small pieces of wood that always move the same way when you push them. People have their own thoughts and ways of being. Because of this, the thinking might not be strong enough to be always true.

english_query: when it is good enough to pick something that works instead of the best thing
ugf_response:
First idea says that when you have a thing that works well enough you can pick that thing even if it is not the very best thing. It tells us that a working thing can be enough for the need we have, and that we do not have to keep looking for the top thing if the one we have already does the job.

Second idea says that from the first idea we can think that we do not need to keep looking for a better thing. It makes us feel that once we have a thing that works we can stop looking and just use that thing. It also leads us to think that we can save time and effort by staying with the thing that works, even when a better thing might be out there somewhere else.

Third idea looks at whether that way of thinking is right. It points out that sometimes it is good to use a thing that works because we may not have time or the things we have to find the top thing. It also shows that the thinking can be not right when the thing that works is only a little bit good and a much better thing could give us far more being not hurt, being well, or a good end. In those cases the thinking may make us miss out on a chance to get a much better end. So the thinking can be good in some situations and not good in others, and we should ask we if the thing we have is enough for what we need or if looking for a better thing would give us a big get more. The best way is to check how important the need is, how much time we have, and how much better the other thing could be, and then decide if a working thing is enough or if we should keep looking for the top thing.

english_query: Either she will take the train or she will take the bus. She will not take the train. Therefore, she will take the bus. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.

english_query: Excellent! You've spotted the core inconsistency. Discuss with the dealer how the tavern could design a fair game that eliminates ambiguity.

Then we think about what the person who runs the game can do to make that small part clear. One way is to write a short line that says exactly what each person must do and what happens, and to use the same chance for each person, like using one die that is thrown the same way for everyone. Another way is to let each person see the same thing at the same time, so no one can say they did not know what was happening. Even if someone says that this makes the game slower, the clear small part makes the game good for all and stops the hard to understand part. The person who runs the game should write one short line that is clear and says exactly what each person does and use the same chance for everyone, so the game is good for all and not hard to understand.

english_query: If the door is unlocked, then the door can be opened from outside. If the door can be opened from outside, then the soup tastes savory. Therefore, if the door is unlocked, then the soup tastes savory. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first idea. It says if the door is not locked, then a person can open it from the outside. Second, we look at the next idea. It says if a person can open the door from the outside, then the food is good to eat. Now we put these two things together. We start with the door being not locked. Because of the first idea, this means the door can be opened from the outside. Because the door can be opened from the outside, the second idea tells us the food is good to eat. So, if we start with the door not locked, we end up with the food being good to eat. The last idea says exactly this: if the door is not locked, the food is good to eat. This fits our steps. We can try a small story to check. Suppose the door is not locked. Then it must be possible to open from outside. Then the food must be good to eat. There is no way for the door to be not locked and the food not to be good to eat, given these two ideas. So the last idea follows for sure. follows

english_query: why who someone is to you changes what you should do for them
ugf_response:
Imagine you see a person who is falling down on the street. You do not know this person. You do not know their name or their life. You might stop to help them or you might keep walking. Now, imagine that the person falling down is your own brother or your own child. Most people would say you must stop and help them right away. The way you act changes because of who that person is to you.

This brings us to a hard question about how we should live. Should we act toward every person in the same way, or should we give more care to some people and less care to others? If we say that every person is the same, then it seems we should help the stranger and the brother in the same way. But if we say that the people we love are more important, then we are saying that some people are more great to us than others.

Some people think that we must act the same for everyone. They believe that if we want a good world, we must not pick those we like more than others. They say that if we only help our friends and our family, we are being not right to the rest of the world. To them, the right way to act is to follow a way of living that stays the same for every person, no matter who they are. They think that if we do things in a way that is not the same for people we love, we are breaking the way things should be.

Other people think that it is right and good to care more for those close to us. They say that love and family are what make us human. They believe that the thing we must do for our own people is a very strong thing that comes before anything else. To them, it is not wrong to help a friend more than a stranger. They think that a world where no one cares more for their own children than for a stranger would be a cold and bad place to live. These people believe that the ties we have to others are what tell us how we should act.

english_query: how to think about what the other person will do before you act
ugf_response:
First, the first idea says that you can see some signs or small signs about what another person might do. Those signs can be things like the way they act, the words they say, or the things they have done before. This part is true because we all notice that people give out signs all the time, even if they do not try to.

Second, the second idea says that from those signs you can guess what the other person will do next. It says that if you see a sign, you can think about what it means and then make a guess about the next move of that person. This is the step that moves from the first idea to a new thought.

Third, the third idea says that because you have that guess, you should change the way you do your own thing. It says that you should wait, hurry, or do something else so that your own thing you do fits with the guess you have made about the other person.

Now look at whether the thinking is good. The part that says you can see signs is strong, so the start of the thought line is on a still ground. The part that says you can turn those signs into a guess can help, but it is not always safe. A sign can be not clear, and a guess can be wrong. When a guess is wrong, the last idea – to change your own thing you do resting on that guess – can lead you to do the wrong thing. So the whole line works well only when the guess is close to what will really happen. If you are not sure, it may be better to keep your own plan simple and be ready to change it later, rather than to move early because of a guess that might be off.

In short, the say that signs are there is true, the say that signs lead to a guess makes sense, and the say that you should do something resting on that guess can be good when the guess is right but can be bad when the guess is wrong. How good the thinking is is about how well you can read the signs and how certain you are about the guess before you change your own thing you do.

english_query: If she studies for the test, then she passes the test. If she passes the test, then the soup tastes savory. Therefore, if she studies for the test, then the soup tastes savory. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.

english_query: how to decide whose good matters and how much
ugf_response:
Imagine a world where you must choose which man or woman to help when you can only help one. You see two people who are in bad trouble. One person is a young child who has many years of life ahead. The other person is an old man who has lived a long time and has done much for other people. You have only enough food or things that make the body well to save one of them. This is a hard thing to think about because it feels wrong to let someone die when you could have saved them.

The big question is how we decide which person is more important. We want to know how to find the size of the good that a person brings to the world. We want to know how much weight to give to the life of a person when we must choose between them. There is no easy way to see this or to count it like we count things we eat or stones.

First, some people might say we should save the young child. They think that because the child has many years left, the child can do many more good things in the future. They look at the time that is left. They think that more time means more good can happen. To them, the part of life left is the most important thing to look at.

Second, other people might say we should save the old man. They think that what a person has already done for the world matters most. The old man has helped many people and has built many things. They believe that the good he has already done is a reason to keep him alive. They look at the past to decide what to do in the now.

Third, some might say that every person is as great as any other. They think it is not for us to say one life is better than another. They believe that we cannot use numbers to find the good in people because every life is a special thing that cannot be known by counting. They think that trying to pick a person who is better is a bad way to live. Each of these ways of thinking has a reason that sounds right, but they all lead to different choices.

So we say there are only two ways to talk about god, since other ways point to how they stand to what they are about, both in their wide and narrow sense; but nothing in god can stand to what it is in or who it is about, except the tie of being the same; and this is because god is so simple.

english_query: the problem of spending more just because you already spent a lot
ugf_response:
Imagine you go to a place to buy things. At first, you do not want to spend much money. You have a small part of your money you want to use. But then, something happens. You buy one thing. Then you buy another thing. Now you have given away a lot of money.

A strange thing happens in your head. You start to think that because you have already given away a lot, you should keep spending more. You feel that the money you already gave away is gone and cannot come back. You think that if you stop now, the money you already gave away was a bad thing that should not have happened. But if you spend even more, you feel like you are making a big choice.

This is a bad way to think. It is a way that makes people lose even more money. You might think that spending more will make the first spending seem good. But it does not. The money you spent at the start is gone. You cannot get it back by spending more.

The right way to think is to look at each new thing you might buy. You should ask yourself if you want this new thing right now. You should not ask if this new thing will make your past spending look good. You should only look at what you have now and what you will have if you spend or do not spend. Each time you spend, it is a new choice. Do not let the money you spent before change how you make your next choice. If you do not want the new thing, do not buy it, no matter how much you have already spent.

english_query: The holographic instructor is a former game‑theorist who loves to expose the tension between formal rationality and intuitive cooperation.
ugf_response: First, we look at the teacher who used to study how people make choices when their wins are tied to what others do. They like to show how clear thinking says one thing, but our deep feeling says another. The big point is that clear thinking often says we should look out for only us to win the most. But our deep feeling says we should work with others to help everyone. The teacher wants us to see this fight between two ways of thinking. They think it is important to know that being good at numbers does not always mean being nice or working together. Some might say we should just follow the number way because it is sure to win. But the teacher says that ignoring the human side of helping others is a bad way to live. So the main point is to make us think about why we help others even when the number way says we should not. The teacher uses old ideas about games to show us that doing what is best for the group often goes against what is best for just one person. This helps us understand the hard choice between being good at numbers and being kind.

Since you do not have the job, you did not win. Not winning is the same as losing. So, the last idea says the person loses the pick. This fits with what we found. So you did not cross the line. This way of thinking holds up. There is no other way for the first two ideas to be true and the last one to be wrong. So, the last thing follows for sure. follows

english_query: why people sometimes pick the worse thing even when they know better
ugf_response:
Sometimes a person knows that one path will lead to a good life and another path will lead to a bad life. They can see the bad path coming from far away. They know it will bring them pain or make them lose what they love. Yet, they walk toward the bad path anyway. They choose the thing that will hurt them. This is a strange thing that happens to many people.

The big question is why this happens. If we know what is good and what is bad, why do we not always choose the good? Why does the mind lead the body to do things that we know are not good for us?

Some people say this happens because our feelings are stronger than our thoughts. A person might feel a strong want right now. This want is like a fire that burns very hot. The thought that the fire will burn them later is like a small, cold wind. The hot want wins. The person wants the way something feels in their mouth or the fast fun now, even if they know it will make them feel bad when the sun goes down.

Other people say this happens because our brains are not made for the world we live in now. Long ago, humans had to find food and stay safe from big animals. In those times, taking what was in front of them was good. Now, we have many things that look good but are not good for us. Our brains still work in the old way. We see something that looks good for a moment and we take it before we can think about the long time ahead.

Some people also say that we do not know the person we are as well as we think. We think we are the ones who have all the power over our own minds. But there are parts of our mind that work without us knowing. These parts can push us toward a bad choice without us feeling it happen. We might think we are making a choice, but we are really just following a path that was already set by things we do not see.

english_query: the problem where two people could work together but might not
ugf_response:
The first idea is that two people can work together to do a thing. This means that if they join their work, the thing will be done well or it will be done. The second idea is that these two people might choose not to work together. This happens because they might think that working alone is better for them, or they might not trust the other person to do their part. The third idea is that this choice can lead to a bad thing where the work does not get done at all.

What follows from these ideas is that even when working together is the best way to reach what they want, people will often not do it. This is because each person looks only at what is good for them in that moment. They think about what they get if they work alone and then they think about what they get if they work with another. This can lead to a situation where the group gets less than what they could have had if they had just joined together.

The way the person thinks about this is good because it shows how people act when they are not thinking about the whole group. It points to a real problem in how people live and work. However, to see if the whole thought is strong, we must look at why they choose not to work together. If the reason is only about what one person gets, then the thought is strong. If there are other reasons, like not knowing how to work together, then the thought might need more parts to be the very same as what is happening in the real world.

Person number two: No, it does not work that way. Saying something many times does not change what is real. The truth stays the same no matter how many times you say it.

Person number one: But why does it feel like it might be true if I hear it all the time?

Person number two: Our brains are strange. When we hear a thing many times, our minds start to feel like it is right. It becomes easy to think about. We take a feeling of things being easy and think it is the feeling of truth.

Person number one: So my mind is playing a game with me?

Person number two: Yes. Your mind sees something many times and says, this must be true because it is so easy to know. But being easy to know is not the same as being real.

Person number one: If I want to know if a thing is true, what should I do instead of just listening to it?

Person number two: You must look at the world to see if the thing is there. You must find things that show the thing is real. Do not trust your mind just because it feels sure. Trust what you can see and what is actually happening.

english_query: the question of what we owe to people we will never meet
ugf_response:
Person 1: I wonder, what should we give to people we will never see?  

Person 2: We can think of it as a need to keep things good for them, like clean air, safe water, and a just chance to live.  

Person 1: But how can we know what they need if we never meet them?  

Person 2: We can look at what helps all of us now and try to keep that going, because what helps us now will also help them later.  

Person 1: Does that mean we have to change how we act today?  

Person 2: Yes, we can try to use less things we throw away, grow more trees, and make laws that keep the world nice for the next ones.  

Person 1: Some people say that is just a feeling, not a real need. What do you think?  

Person 2: I think it is a real need because if we take too much now, there will be less for those who come after, and that can make life hard for them.  

Person 1: So the thing we need to give is to keep the world in a good shape?  

Person 2: Yes, we need to keep the world in a shape that lets the next ones live, learn, and be happy.

english_query: The wager correctly illustrates how infinite utilities can dominate expected‑utility calculations, highlighting a limitation of the MEU framework when faced with unbounded payoffs. It serves as a useful pedagogical tool for discussing the boundaries of rational decision‑making.
ugf_response: First, we look at a game made by a man. He said if you think God is real, you get forever of good times. If you are wrong, you lose little. The number work says you should pick the big win. Then, we think about the way we pick the best thing to do. This way works well when wins and bad things are normal sizes. But here, the win is without end. This breaks the number work because without end is bigger than any number we can count. So, the first idea is that this game shows the way of picking has a hole when things get too big. Someone might say that forever is not real, so the number work does not matter. They might say we should not use made up numbers for real life. Even so, the point is not if God is real, but if the number work works. The number work fails to give a clear answer when the good thing has no stop. This means the way of picking is not perfect for all cases. It is a good way for teaching because it shows where the way stops working. It helps us see that we need new ideas for very big chances. So, the words are true because they point out a real problem in the number work of choice. The game shows that the usual way for good choices breaks down when good things are without end, making it a good way to teach about where that way stops working.

english_query: Also, in things made by one who wants to make them, and whose want is not caused by anything, there can be no cause given except by the want of him who wants. But the want of God is the cause of all things, as was shown before. If, then, there is no cause of His want, we cannot look in any things of the world for any cause, except the want of God alone. So all knowing would be for nothing, since knowing looks to give causes to what happens. This seems wrong, and so we must give some cause to the want of God.
ugf_response: Since God wants what happens to come from certain causes, to keep order in the world, it is not wrong to look for causes that are after the want of God. It would be wrong to do so, if such were seen as first, and not as resting on the want of God. In this way the great old church teacher says: 'Men who think they know much have chosen to put chance things to other causes, being not able at all to see the cause that is shown above all others, the want of God.'

english_query: A game tree displays a sequential game with decision nodes (who moves), branches (available actions), terminal nodes (outcomes with payoffs), and information sets (what each player knows at their move). In this hideout scenario the Hero moves first, then the Bandit Leader reacts, making timing crucial.
ugf_response: First, we see the first person moves and the second person sees that move. The second person knows what the first person did. This means the second person can pick the best answer for themselves. The first person knows this will happen. So the first person must think about what the second person will do. If the first person picks a bad path, the second person will make it worse. The first person should pick a path that is safe even if the second person tries to hurt them. The second person will always try to get the most good for themselves because they move last. The first person must pick a move that stays good no matter what the second person does, and the second person will pick the move that helps them the most.

english_query: how a person can change who they are by changing what they do
ugf_response:
A person can look at the picture in their mind of who the person inside is. They may think that they are quiet, not loud, because they have done those things for a long time. One day they start to do new things – they speak up in a group, they help a stranger, they try a game they never tried before. As they keep doing these new things, the way they see the person inside can change. The picture in their mind of what they are can grow bigger or turn to a different shape.

The hard question that comes up is whether a person really becomes a different person when they change what they do, or whether they are only adding new parts to the same old person inside. One side of the question asks if the inside – the feelings, the thoughts, the sense of who they are – follows the outside things they do. The other side asks if the inside can stay the same even while the outside changes a lot. A third view says that the inside and the outside are tied together, so a change in one will always pull the other along, but not always in the same how much.

Many people say that the answer is yes, that who we are is mostly what we do. They point to the fact that when we act in a new way we learn new things they can do well, meet new people, and feel new feelings, and those new pieces become part of the picture of the person inside. They think that the more we do a certain way the more that way becomes part of who we are.

Other people say that the answer is no, that a deep part of the inside can stay the same even if we try many new things. They talk about a deep feeling of “I am still me” that does not move just because we pick up a new thing for fun or speak in a new voice. For them, the inside is like a quiet river that can move like water under many different stones without being changed by the stones.

A third group says that both ideas have truth. They say that some parts of the inside can change with new things they do, especially the parts that are about how we see the world and what we think we can do. At the same time, they say there are parts that stay the same, like a simple wish to be kind or a simple need to feel safe. The way what changes and what stays are put together can be different for each person, which is why the question is hard and why many answers can be given.

english_query: And do you not agree in that way of seeing things, old teacher?
ugf_response: If you do, my friend; but I want to know first, whether you say that breaking all things down into their small parts is a good way to explain them, and looking at them as letters or bigger groups of letters is not a good way--is this your way of seeing things?

english_query: Also, a special thing is what belongs to one alone. Since there are more than one in God who come from another, there is nothing to stop several from not coming from another. So the Father is not the only one who does not come from another.
ugf_response: In every kind of thing there must be something first; so in what God is, there must be one start which is not from another, and we call this not coming from another. To say there are two such starts is to say there are two Gods and two ways of being God. So an old teacher of the church says: As there is one God, so there cannot be two who do not come from another. And this is especially true because, if there were two who do not come from another, one would not be from the other, and they would not be different by how they stand to each other: so they would be different by having different ways of being.

english_query: Also, where there is a order, there is also a way things are not the same. But there is no way things are not the same in the god way of being. So it does not have a order; and a order of how things come to be is not in it.
ugf_response: The order of how things come to be does not mean putting the way of being itself in a line, but means that there is an order in the god persons because of how they start to be.

english_query: Either she will take the train or she will take the bus. She will take the train. Therefore, she will not take the bus. Is this argument valid or invalid? Explain your reasoning.
ugf_response: First, we look at the first line. It says one of two things must happen. Either she goes on the train or she goes on the bus. This means at least one of these choices is true. It does not say that only one can be true. Next, we look at the second line. It tells us for sure that she will take the train. This fits with the first part of the first idea. Now we check the last line. It says she will not take the bus. Does the first idea stop her from taking both? No. The first idea just says she takes the train or the bus. It allows for the chance that she takes both. Let us think of a small story. In this story, she takes the train to a place and then takes the bus from there. In this story, she takes the train, so the second line is true. She also takes the bus, so the first line is true because at least one happened. But the last line is wrong because she did take the bus. Since we can imagine a story where the first two lines are true but the last one is not, the last idea does not follow for sure. does not follow

english_query: what makes someone happy in the deepest way, not just for a moment
ugf_response: First we think about the feeling that comes when we eat good food or get a new thing to play with. This feeling is nice but it goes away fast. Then we look for the feeling that stays with us for a long time. We see that this deep feeling comes from being close to other people who care for us. It also comes from doing work that feels good and makes us feel like we are doing something good for the world. When we help others or learn new things we feel strong and true. This kind of good feeling does not fade like the good feeling from a sweet thing to eat. It grows when we share it with friends and family. So the deepest good feeling is not just about getting what we want right now. It is about having good ties to other people and feeling like our life has a good reason for being.

english_query: why saying something over and over does not make it true
ugf_response: First, the first idea says that a fact does not change just because a person says the same words many times. Second, the second idea says that many people think the other way: they think that hearing a thing a lot can make it feel true in the mind, so they may start to believe it even if it does not fit with what really is. Third, the third idea says that this is a wrong idea, because the truth of a thing needs what actually happened or what can be shown, not how often the thing is spoken. From these ideas the person thinks that we should not let the number of times a thing is spoken decide whether we accept it as true. They think we should check the thing against things we can see or know, and we should be careful not to be pulled just by hearing it a lot. This line of thinking is good because it points out a normal wrong thing that can lead people to believe things that are not right. The real check of a thing is not how loud or how often it is said, but whether it fits with what we can see, hear, or count.

english_query: why following rules is not enough if you do not care about people
ugf_response:
The person is telling us something about how we act. The first idea is that there are ways of acting that are set down for us to follow. These are the ways we are told we must act. The second idea is that some people do not care about the well being or the feelings of other people. The third idea is that if you only follow these ways of acting but you do not care about other people, you are not doing what is right.

The person thinks that if you only do what you are told to do, you might still do things that are bad. They think that if you do not have care for others in your heart, then just following the ways of acting is not enough to make you a good person. They think that the good way to live must have both following the ways of acting and caring for the people around you.

We must look at if this way of thinking is good. The first part is true. It is true that there are ways of acting that we follow. It is also true that some people do not care about others. However, we must ask if the way they put these things together is right. If a person follows every way of acting but does not care about people, they might still do things that cause hurt. This is because the ways of acting cannot tell you what to do in every single moment. Sometimes the ways of acting are not enough to stop bad things from happening. For this reason, the person is right to say that caring for people is a thing that must be added to the ways of acting. The way they think is good because it shows that being good is more than just doing what you are told.

english_query: how to pick the best choice when you are not sure what will happen
ugf_response:
Person 1: I do not know what to do next because I am not sure what will happen. How do you pick the best choice?

Person 2: First, you must think about what you want most. Then you look at the things that might happen if you pick each way.

Person 1: But I do not know which thing will happen. How can I look at things that are not here yet?

Person 2: You guess what has a good chance to happen. You think about the good parts and the bad parts of each guess.

Person 1: What if my guess is wrong? How do I know I picked the right one?

Person 2: You pick the one where the bad parts are not too bad and the good parts are very good. You try to make the bad things less sure to happen.

Person 1: So I should not just hope for the best? I should plan for the bad too?

Person 2: Yes. If you plan for the bad, you are ready if it comes. If it does not come, you are happy. This makes the choice safer for you.

english_query: Also, things in one group seem to be of one kind. But the great old church teacher puts the feeling part against the high and low thinking parts, which are about knowing. So the feeling part is also about knowing.
ugf_response: The feeling part is put against high and low thinking because they all share the act of moving: for the power of knowing, which has high and low thinking, is a power that moves; as is the power of wanting, which has the feeling part.

english_query: Dominance can only eliminate strategies; it does not always resolve a game. Some games have no strictly dominated strategies, requiring Nash equilibrium or other analytical tools.
ugf_response: First, we look at how people pick their moves in a game. One way is to throw away any choice that is always worse than another choice, no matter what the other person does. If you do this again, you might be left with only one move. If that happens, the game is done. But often, this stops before only one move is left. In those cases, every move left has some good points and some bad points, looking at what the other person picks. So, just throwing away bad moves is not enough. We need a different way to think, like looking for a spot where no one wants to change their move if they know what the other person is doing. This spot is a place where everyone is happy to stay put. Without this idea, we cannot always find the answer just by cutting out the worst choices. It is true that cutting out bad moves does not always finish the game. When no moves are clearly the worst, we must use other ideas, like finding a place where no one wants to change, to figure out what will happen.

english_query: Review the tree again. Notice which branch leads to Brick's decision point and ask yourself: What does Brick see before he chooses?
ugf_response: First, we look at the drawing of the game. We find the spot where the second person must pick a path. We look at the line that leads to this spot. This line shows what happened before his turn. The first person made a move. The second person sees this first move. He knows what the first person did. Some might think he sees all possible ends of the game. But he only sees what has already happened. He does not know what will happen later. He only knows the past steps. The path to his spot shows the first choice. If the first person went one way, the second person sees that way. This is how these games work. You see the history. You do not see the future. So, the second person sees the move made by the person before him. This is the only thing he can use to think. The second person sees the move made by the first player. This is the only thing he knows when it is his turn.
